$comment: SOURCE OF TRUTH for the Backend & API contract. ONE contract holds endpoint shape, error envelope, auth/session, pagination, and telemetry as rules. chattr backend is Python FastAPI (chat server + MCP bridge + agent wrappers). Rules locked only as a migrated slice demands them.
title: Backend & API
summary: The FastAPI surface and its conventions: endpoint definitions and routing, a single error-envelope shape, auth / session validation, pagination, the WebSocket event vocabulary, and telemetry / logging. Localhost-only security posture (session token + loopback registration + origin checks) carried forward from legacy chattr.

rules.text: HTTP/WS routes live in `app/api/` modules and are registered on the app factory in `app/main.py`. System/operational routes (e.g. `/health`, `/ws`) are unprefixed; product routes are mounted under `/v1`.
Every error response uses one shape: `{"error": {"code": "<machine_code>", "message": "<human message>"}}`.

enforcedBy: contract tests / OpenAPI diff + ast-grep — derived from these rules
enforcement: Endpoint rule (pending) — routes follow the agreed router/path layout.
Error-envelope rule (pending) — all error responses match one shape.
openItems: Auth / session: how the server validates the session token — define and lock.
WebSocket event vocabulary (agents, agent_renamed, status, clear, etc.) — define and lock.
Localhost security posture (session token, loopback-only registration, origin checks, no shell=True) — confirm and lock.
Telemetry & logging conventions — define and lock.
